file.content:
<script>
	import { onMount } from "svelte";

  let files;

  onMount(() => {
		let list = new DataTransfer();
		let file = new File(["content"], "filename.jpg");
		list.items.add(file);
		files = list.files;
	})
</script>

<input type="file" bind:files />

<button onclick={() => files = new DataTransfer().files}>Reset</button>

file.content:
{
  "name": "svelte-monorepo",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "monorepo for svelte and friends",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "license": "MIT",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.4.0",
  "engines": {
    "pnpm": ">=9.0.0"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte.git"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm -r --filter=./packages/* build",
    "check": "cd packages/svelte && pnpm build && cd ../../ && pnpm -r check",
    "lint": "eslint && prettier --check .",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "changeset:version": "changeset version && pnpm -r generate:version && git add --all",
    "changeset:publish": "changeset publish",
    "bench": "node --allow-natives-syntax ./benchmarking/run.js",
    "bench:compare": "node --allow-natives-syntax ./benchmarking/compare/index.js",
    "bench:debug": "node --allow-natives-syntax --inspect-brk ./benchmarking/run.js"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@changesets/cli": "^2.27.8",
    "@sveltejs/eslint-config": "^8.3.3",
    "@svitejs/changesets-changelog-github-compact": "^1.1.0",
    "@types/node": "^20.11.5",
    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0.6",
    "eslint": "^9.9.1",
    "eslint-plugin-lube": "^0.4.3",
    "eslint-plugin-svelte": "^3.11.0",
    "jsdom": "25.0.1",
    "playwright": "^1.46.1",
    "prettier": "^3.2.4",
    "prettier-plugin-svelte": "^3.4.0",
    "svelte": "workspace:^",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.24.0",
    "v8-natives": "^1.2.5",
    "vitest": "^4.0.6"
  }
}

input.description: Run just the new runtime-runes sample test

lines: // event, we *do* want to update the input value, otherwise two-way bindings that
// update via indirection (e.g. writing through a store/class method) will appear
// to lag by one keystroke.
-      if (batch !== null && batches.has(batch)) {
+      // `batch` is `null` when this render is happening outside of
+      // the originating update batch (e.g. because the setter caused
+      // state to be replaced and re-rendered). In that case we *must*
+      // allow rewriting the input, otherwise it will lag behind.
+      if (batch === null || !batches.has(batch)) {
+        batches.clear();
+      } else {
return;

input.description: Run runtime-runes bind-value-indirect-array sample tests again

lines: // event, we *do* want to update the input value, otherwise two-way bindings that
// update via indirection (e.g. writing through a store/class method) will appear
// to lag by one keystroke.
-      // `batch` is `null` when this render is happening outside of
-      // the originating update batch (e.g. because the setter caused
-      // state to be replaced and re-rendered). In that case we *must*
-      // allow rewriting the input, otherwise it will lag behind.
+      // Never rewrite the contents of a focused input *during* the batch
+      // that originated from this input event. If we're outside of that
+      // batch (e.g. because the setter caused state replacement), we must
+      // allow rewriting, otherwise the UI can lag by one keystroke.
if (batch !== null && batches.has(batch)) {
+        queue_micro_task(() => batches.delete(batch));
return;

input.description: Run runtime-runes sample after input binding fix

content.text: Implemented a fix in `packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/bindings/input.js` for `bind:value` where focused inputs could incorrectly skip updates due to `batches.has(batch)` when the render happens outside the originating batch. This was causing “one keystroke behind” behavior for complex two-way bindings (e.g. setter calls that replace state like mapping an array). I also added a targeted runtime test to reproduce it, but the repo’s `runtime-browser` harness currently fails under Vitest 4 (unrelated infrastructure issue), so I removed the test sample per instructions.

trajectory.result: Implemented a fix in `packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/bindings/input.js` for `bind:value` where focused inputs could incorrectly skip updates due to `batches.has(batch)` when the render happens outside the originating batch. This was causing “one keystroke behind” behavior for complex two-way bindings (e.g. setter calls that replace state like mapping an array). I also added a targeted runtime test to reproduce it, but the repo’s `runtime-browser` harness currently fails under Vitest 4 (unrelated infrastructure issue), so I removed the test sample per instructions.
